name: pitfalls-react
description: React component patterns, forms, accessibility, and responsive design. Use when building React components, handling forms, or ensuring accessibility. Triggers on: React component, useEffect, form validation, a11y, responsive, Error Boundary.
capabilities: Provides React component patterns and code examples
Shows proper form validation with Zod and React Hook Form
Guides error boundary implementation for graceful degradation
Covers accessibility best practices with ARIA labels
author: BarisSozen
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance on React best practices. All 33 static findings are false positives from keyword pattern matching on documentation examples. No executable code, no file access, no network calls, no external commands.
valueStatement: React developers often encounter common mistakes that cause bugs and accessibility issues. This skill provides proven patterns for components, forms, error handling, and responsive design.
userTitle: Avoid React Pitfalls with Best Practices
limitations: Does not execute or test React code
Does not modify existing project files
Does not generate complete React applications from scratch
Does not access external APIs or documentation

useCases.title: Review React Components
useCases.description: Review existing React components against best practice patterns for quality and accessibility.

useCases.title: Learn Correct Patterns
useCases.description: Learn proper React patterns including hooks, state management, and error handling from examples.

useCases.title: Build Accessible Forms
useCases.description: Implement form validation with proper error handling and accessibility for React applications.

promptTemplates.title: Component Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this React component code and identify any anti-patterns or mistakes according to React best practices. Check for proper loading/error states, data checks, useEffect usage, and accessibility issues.

promptTemplates.title: Form Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement a form with proper validation using Zod schemas and React Hook Form. Include inline error display and disabled submit state while validating.

promptTemplates.title: Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an error boundary component for wrapping major sections of my React app. Include proper error logging and a retry mechanism for graceful degradation.

promptTemplates.title: Accessibility Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my React component for accessibility issues. Check for ARIA labels, keyboard navigation support, semantic HTML, and focus indicators.

bestPractices: Always check data existence before accessing properties to prevent runtime errors
Implement proper loading and error states with separate UI components
Use semantic HTML elements and ARIA labels for accessibility
antiPatterns: Defining functions inside JSX render or onClick handlers
Using window.location.href for navigation instead of router Link
Accessing data properties without null checks on optional data

faq.question: Which React versions are supported?
faq.answer: Patterns apply to React 16.8+ with hooks. Class components shown for error boundaries are still valid.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance and code examples. It does not execute code or modify your project files.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes, patterns work with Next.js. Use Link from next/link for navigation instead of wouter.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this is a read-only guidance skill. It does not access files, network, or system resources.

faq.question: Why am I not getting responses?
faq.answer: Ensure your prompt includes React keywords like component, useEffect, form, or accessibility for triggering.

faq.question: How is this different from React documentation?
faq.answer: This skill provides practical patterns and common pitfalls. Official docs cover API reference.
